source.title: Sleep Disturbance Forecasts Beta-Amyloid Accumulation across Subsequent Years.

confidence_rationale: Direct prospective test of whether PSG-measured sleep architecture forecasts a biological intermediate (cortical amyloid accumulation) for Alzheimer pathology, in a clinically normal older adult cohort with longitudinal PiB-PET tracking. The proportion of NREM slow-wave activity below 1Hz and sleep efficiency both predicted the slope of subsequent amyloid deposition. Crucially, the paper tests specificity -- no other macro- or micro-architecture metric showed forecasting sensitivity -- which is exactly the falsification structure Q-032's PD-biomarker proposal needs. Held below 0.85 because the cohort is moderately sized, AB accumulation is a biological surrogate for dementia outcome (not the outcome itself), and the SWA <1Hz subset is a microarchitecture metric not routinely captured in clinical PSG.
failure_signatures: Beta-amyloid accumulation rate is a biological surrogate, not the clinical dementia outcome Q-032 proposes as the trial endpoint. Translating AB-accumulation forecasting into dementia-outcome forecasting is a separate inferential step.

The forecasting signal localised to NREM SWA below 1Hz, not total SWS%. Clinical PSG typically reports macroscopic stage percentages, not the <1Hz subband. Q-032's PD-biomarker proposal would need to specify whether the biomarker is total SWS% (clinically captured) or SWA <1Hz (research-grade only).

The cohort were clinically normal adults; the paper does not test whether the same architecture-AB relationship holds in MCI or in pharmacologically-suppressed-SWS samples where the upstream cause is exogenous.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Baseline PSG measures of NREM slow-wave activity (proportion of SWA below 1Hz) and sleep efficiency prospectively predict the rate (slope) of cortical beta-amyloid deposition measured longitudinally with [11C]PiB-PET imaging in clinically normal older adults, controlling for age, sex, and sleep apnea. Other macro- and microarchitecture metrics did NOT show this forecasting sensitivity, suggesting the SWA <1Hz signal is specific.
mapping.ree_translation: Q-032 limb (c): supports the architecture-specific PD-biomarker proposal. The Winer/Mander/Walker test design -- prospectively forecasting a biological intermediate of dementia from PSG architecture, with explicit specificity testing across other architecture metrics -- is the strongest possible methodological template for what Q-032 needs in a trial setting. The fact that SWA <1Hz specifically (and not e.g. spindle density or REM%) carries the forecasting signal is consistent with the REE pipeline-phase model where Phase 1/2 (SHY homeostasis + schema replay, MECH-120/121) sit upstream of amyloid clearance via the glymphatic mechanism.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Two transfer steps separate this finding from Q-032. First, AB accumulation is a biological surrogate for clinical dementia, not the dementia outcome itself. Second, the forecasting signal localised to NREM SWA below 1Hz, which is a research-grade microarchitecture metric not captured by routine clinical PSG. Treating clinically-reported SWS% as equivalent to research SWA <1Hz is a methodological leap that would need to be addressed in any Q-032 trial design.
mapping.source_context: Cohort of clinically normal older adults assessed with overnight PSG and longitudinal [11C]PiB-PET imaging tracking cortical amyloid deposition over subsequent years. Specificity controls included macro-architecture stages (N1, N2, N3, REM) and other microarchitecture metrics (sleep efficiency, spindles, etc.).

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high: Current Biology, longitudinal PET tracking, prospective forecasting design with explicit specificity testing. Mapping fidelity high but not perfect because AB-accumulation is a biological surrogate. Transfer risk moderate because (i) AB-accumulation does not equal dementia outcome and (ii) SWA <1Hz is a microarchitecture metric requiring research-grade scoring.
